xccdf:Benchmark.xccdf:title: Sanitized Chrome for Windows STIG-Style Example

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection must be enabled

xccdf:description.xccdf:VulnDiscussion: Browser protections help reduce exposure to known malicious sites, suspicious downloads, and unsafe extensions.
xccdf:description.xccdf:Mitigations: Document any approved exception to the enterprise browser policy baseline.

xccdf:check.xccdf:check-content: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel is set to enhanced protection.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Configure Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection through Group Policy or the registry policy path and document the applied baseline.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: Chrome password manager must be disabled

xccdf:check.xccdf:check-content: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing PasswordManagerEnabled is disabled for managed users.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Disable Chrome Password Manager using enterprise policy.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: Chrome extension installation must be restricted

xccdf:check.xccdf:check-content: Review Chrome enterprise extension policy and confirm unapproved extension installation is blocked or allowlisted.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Configure Chrome ExtensionInstallBlocklist and ExtensionInstallAllowlist policies through GPO or registry.
